Home.metaDescription: All current drug shortages in Switzerland — updated daily from drugshortage.ch and BWL. Search by active substance, manufacturer or ATC code. Publicly accessible, no registration required.
Home.step1Body: The latest shortage notifications from every official Swiss source — reconciled daily, so you don't have to check three portals yourself.
Home.step2Body: Active substance, ATC code and manufacturer are automatically attached. No manual lookups, no copy-paste between systems.
Home.step3Body: Full-text search, filters by manufacturer or ATC group, a detail page per product. Publicly accessible, no registration required.
Home.dataItem4Desc: Weekly view of how shortages evolve
Home.searchItem4Desc: Products with the same active substance

Faq.title: Frequently asked questions
Faq.faq1A: engpass.radar is an independent project by Henrik Rühe, a neurologist in Switzerland. No pharma sponsor, no advertising, no affiliate links. Built out of clinical need and run in his free time. The source code is publicly available on GitHub.
Faq.faq2Q: Where does the data come from?
Faq.faq2A: Shortage notifications come primarily from drugshortage.ch. Data from the Federal Office for National Economic Supply (BWL) and the shortage list of the hospital pharmacy at the University Hospital Basel are added on top. Active substance, ATC code, Swissmedic number and GTIN come from the public ODDB reference database.
Faq.faq3A: The data is reconciled automatically every day. The header shows when the last import ran. If a sync ever fails, the timestamp makes it visible — no stale state is presented as current.
Faq.faq4A: engpass.radar shows what is reported in the aggregated sources — nothing more, nothing less. Unreported shortages will not appear here. For information that is regulatory in nature, the primary source is always authoritative.
Faq.faq5ARich: The dashboard, search, detail pages, CSV export and the basic API are publicly accessible. Higher API quotas, white-label integrations and SLA-backed Institutional plans are paid — these are aimed at software vendors and hospital pharmacy chains. Details at <api>/api</api>.
Faq.faq5APlain: The dashboard, search, detail pages, CSV export and the basic API are publicly accessible. Higher API quotas, white-label integrations and SLA-backed Institutional plans are paid — these are aimed at software vendors and hospital pharmacy chains. Details at /api.
Faq.faq6Q: Can I reuse the data or integrate it into my own system?
Faq.faq6ARich: Yes. A CSV export is available, and a public REST API lives at <docs>engpassradar.ch/api-docs</docs>. The free tier works without an account. For productive integrations, Professional and Institutional plans offer higher quotas and an SLA.
Faq.faq6APlain: Yes. A CSV export is available, and a public REST API lives at engpassradar.ch/api-docs. The free tier works without an account. For productive integrations, Professional and Institutional plans offer higher quotas and an SLA.
Faq.faq7Q: May I use engpass.radar in hospital or pharmacy operations?
Faq.faq7A: Yes — as an information and monitoring tool. For pharmaceutical or clinical decisions, the primary source (drugshortage.ch, Swissmedic, BWL) is authoritative. The monthly report and the Engpass-Signal newsletter are citable and may be circulated internally.
Faq.faq8A: engpass.radar is an information tool, not an official register. The display, score and alternative hints are aggregated automatically; errors in the primary source are passed through unchanged. For clinical, pharmaceutical or regulatory decisions, only the official sources apply.

HomeStructuredData.organizationDescription: Aggregated overview of all reported drug shortages in Switzerland — updated daily.
HomeStructuredData.datasetDescription: Daily aggregation of all officially reported drug shortages in Switzerland. Sources: drugshortage.ch, BWL mandatory stockpile, USB hospital pharmacy. Enriched with active substance (ATC), GTIN, Swissmedic number and Severity Score.

Wirkstoff.metaDescription: All drug shortages for {substanz} in Switzerland. ATC code {atc}. Updated daily.
Wirkstoff.jsonLdDescription: {count, plural, one {# product is} other {# products are}} currently unavailable in Switzerland with the active substance {substanz}.
Wirkstoff.headlineSummary: {count, plural, one {# product is} other {# products are}} currently unavailable in Switzerland.
Wirkstoff.productCountFiltered: {count, plural, one {# product} other {# products}} (filtered from {total})
Wirkstoff.footerSourceFull: Shortage data: drugshortage.ch · Product catalogue: ODDB / Swissmedic · no warranty of completeness
Wirkstoff.footerSourceShortagesOnly: Shortage data: drugshortage.ch · no warranty of completeness

Medikament.metaDescriptionWithSubstanz: {name} drug shortage Switzerland ({substanz}) by {firma}. Current status, alternatives and history at engpassradar.ch.
Medikament.metaDescriptionNoSubstanz: {name} drug shortage Switzerland by {firma}. Current status, alternatives and history at engpassradar.ch.
Medikament.historyIntro: This product (or one with the same active substance) has already been
Medikament.footerSource: Source: drugshortage.ch · no warranty of completeness
Medikament.scoreBarCriticalYes: Product on the BWL mandatory-stockpile list.
Medikament.scoreFootnote: Proprietary index — higher = more severe shortage.

Firma.metaDescription: Drug-shortage profile of {firma}: current shortages, reporting behaviour (Status 1–5) and Severity Score. Updated daily from drugshortage.ch.
Firma.jsonLdDescription: Drug-shortage profile of {firma}: current shortages and Severity Score.
Firma.reportingFootnote: Status 1 = company communicates proactively · Status 4 = no information. A high share of Status 4 raises the Severity Score.

Gtin.metaDescriptionBase: {name}{substanz} — GTIN {gtin}{status}. Swissmedic product data, price and authorisation status at engpassradar.ch.
Gtin.footerSource: Data from ODDB / Swissmedic · no warranty of completeness
